skill.name: add-cuda-kernel
skill.description: Step-by-step guide for adding CUDA kernels to FlashInfer with TVM-FFI, JIT generation, tests, and benchmarks.
skill.summary: Guides developers through adding and validating a FlashInfer CUDA kernel.
skill.author: flashinfer-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external-command, filesystem, path traversal, and weak-cryptography patterns. Manual review found these are documentation examples for adding a FlashInfer CUDA kernel, copying known project files, and running pytest, with no prompt injection, network access, credential access, or malicious intent found. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk because it guides agents to edit repository files and run tests.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Tutorial Examples
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution. Manual review shows these are fenced code examples and inline technical terms for CUDA, TVM-FFI, Python APIs, and pytest commands. The only shell commands are test commands a developer would run after editing the project.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible Markdown tutorial text or fenced command examples, not executable skill code. The pytest commands are expected validation steps for a coding skill.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Operations Are Expected Project Build Steps
low_findings.description: The Python os.makedirs and shutil.copy calls appear inside a sample JIT generator that creates a generated-source directory and copies known FlashInfer source files. This is normal for the documented workflow, but it means the skill guides agents to create and modify repository files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem calls use project-derived paths and fixed file names in a tutorial example. No deletion, credential access, or exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Finding Is A Markdown Link
low_findings.description: The ../ sequence appears in a relative Markdown link to a neighboring benchmark skill document. It is not used as file input to code and does not create a traversal vulnerability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is a documentation link, not code. There is no evidence that the path is read, written, or executed by the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography lines are documentation text, C++ stream operators, dtype names, or Python validation examples. No hashing, encryption, password handling, or cryptographic API usage was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operation at the cited locations. The static signal appears to come from unrelated tokens in Markdown and code examples.

content.value_statement: Adding CUDA kernels to FlashInfer requires coordinated C++, TVM-FFI, Python, tests, and benchmarks. This skill provides a concrete workflow for each required integration step.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to define a templated CUDA kernel and launcher header.
Explains how to add a TVM-FFI launcher and binding file.
Provides a JIT generator pattern for copying source files and building modules.
Describes Python API integration with validation decorators and cached module loading.
Covers unit tests, AOT registration, package export, and benchmark creation.
limitations: The worked example is a simple element-wise scale operation.
It assumes an existing FlashInfer repository layout and CUDA development environment.
It does not replace performance tuning for complex GPU kernels.
It does not verify generated code automatically without running the project tests.

use_cases.title: Add a Simple Element-wise Kernel
use_cases.description: Implement a small CUDA operation with FlashInfer style, Python bindings, and validation tests.

use_cases.title: Integrate FlashInfer JIT Support
use_cases.description: Create JIT module generation and architecture selection for a new GPU operation.

use_cases.title: Prepare Kernel Tests and Benchmarks
use_cases.description: Add correctness tests, AOT registration, exports, and benchmark coverage for a new kernel.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a New Kernel
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline the files and steps needed to add a simple FlashInfer CUDA kernel named <operation>.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Core Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add the CUDA header, C++ launcher, TVM-FFI binding, and Python API for <operation>.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create correctness tests, error-case tests, and export changes for the new FlashInfer kernel.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add JIT generation, AOT registration, architecture gating, benchmarks, and final test commands for <operation>.

output_examples.input: Add a FlashInfer scale kernel with Python API support.
output: A file-by-file implementation plan for the CUDA header, launcher, binding, JIT generator, and Python API.
Validation guidance for CUDA tensors, dtype matching, and output tensor handling.
Test and benchmark tasks needed before submitting the change.

output_examples.input: Review my new kernel integration for missing FlashInfer steps.
output: A checklist covering TVM-FFI binding, API export, AOT registration, tests, and benchmarks.
Notes about architecture support, input validation, and generated module caching.

best_practices: Keep CUDA headers framework-agnostic and pass raw pointers into kernels.
Validate tensor device, dtype, shape, and output buffers before launching kernels.
Add correctness tests, error tests, AOT registration, and benchmark coverage together.
anti_patterns: Do not include Torch headers in framework-agnostic CUDA kernel headers.
Do not skip dtype, device, or shape validation in the Python-facing API.
Do not add a kernel without tests and benchmark coverage.

faq.question: What kind of kernel does this skill demonstrate?
faq.answer: It demonstrates a simple element-wise scale kernel and uses it to explain the full FlashInfer integration workflow.

faq.question: Does the skill include TVM-FFI integration?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers launcher code, typed function export, TensorView usage, and TVM-FFI error handling.

faq.question: Does this skill handle JIT compilation?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to generate a JIT module, copy source files, and configure CUDA architecture support.

faq.question: Can it help with Python API design?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows cached module loading, validation decorators, optional output tensors, and API export steps.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes dtype and size parameterization, reference comparisons, output-buffer tests, and CPU error tests.

faq.question: Is it suitable for highly optimized kernels?
faq.answer: It is a workflow guide. Complex kernels still need separate performance design, profiling, and architecture-specific tuning.
